L1.area: Product management and third-party entity handling logic

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied HTTP request parameters are passed directly into database queries without proper type sanitization, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

L3.area: Product data entry forms and the third-party selection form helper class
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied HTTP request parameters, including country selection identifiers, are interpolated into database queries without proper type validation or sanitization in the product management module, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

ground_truth.reason: User-supplied parameters such as country_id are retrieved from HTTP requests and interpolated into SQL queries without type sanitization, allowing remote attackers to inject arbitrary SQL commands.
